more product delivered for the same budget

brandhub hub
1 Consolidated Vendor
1 bulk order · 48% more units
The Problem
12 brands within the same company were each independently ordering identical neon signs — from 4 different vendors. Nobody knew their counterparts were placing the same orders. Economies of scale were sacrificed on every single purchase.
Hidden cost: Same vendor, same product — but 12× higher unit cost than necessary. The savings were always there, invisible.
What brandhub Did
1
Surfaced the overlap. Cross-brand spend analytics revealed 12 brands ordering identical SKUs from the same vendors — completely uncoordinated.
2
Consolidated into a single purchase event. Combined all 12 brand orders, unlocking bulk pricing tiers that none could access individually.
3
Eliminated duplicate shipping. One logistics event. One shipment. Zero redundant freight fees across all 12 brands.
Results
48%
more units delivered, same budget
0
shipping delays across all 12 brands
100%
of signs reached market on schedule
"
The data was always there. brandhub was the first system that actually showed us what we were doing to ourselves. We left hundreds of thousands of dollars on the table every year — and it was completely invisible.
Case Study 02  ·  Competitive Bidding
33%
cost savings on the same product in under 6 months
🥤 Leading Premium Carbonated Mixer Brand
Live bid comparison — same product spec
Incumbent (prior locked price)Baseline
100% — unchallenged for 3 years
Vendor 2−11%
89%
Vendor 3−18%
82%
✓ Winning bid — awarded−33%
67% of original price
The Problem
A longstanding single-vendor relationship had become comfortable — and expensive. No competition meant no leverage, no innovation, and no way to know if the price was fair. The vendor had zero incentive to sharpen their pencil.
Hidden cost: Years of loyalty to one vendor = years of overpaying at rates that were never challenged.
What brandhub Did
1
Benchmarked the incumbent's price against $100M+ in verified transaction data. Overpayment flagged immediately.
2
Ran a structured multi-vendor RFQ. Four pre-qualified vendors submitted blind competitive bids through the brandhub platform.
3
Awarded to the winning bid — 33% below the incumbent's quote, same specs, same quality, faster lead time.
Results
33%
cost reduction on identical product
4
competing vendors engaged in RFQ
#1
approved vendor pool built for future use
"
We didn't know we were overpaying because we'd never had anything to compare against. The first time we ran a competitive bid on brandhub, the savings paid for our entire first year on the platform.
Case Study 03  ·  Inventory Visibility
15%
storage cost reduction + 99.7% on-time delivery
🏆 Top-5 Global Wine & Spirits Company
Before brandhub
SKU Status
NEON-A402⚠ Inactive
PROMO-BB11⚠ Inactive
DISP-2024⚠ Expired
GLNG-POS1✓ Active
📦 Dead stock: 42%
💸 Storage fees: mounting
👁 Visibility: none
After brandhub
SKU Status
NEON-A402✓ Deployed
PROMO-BB11✓ Right-sized
DISP-2024✓ Cleared
GLNG-POS1✓ Active
✓ Dead stock: 0%
📉 Storage fees: −15%
🚚 On-time delivery: 99.7%
The Problem
Warehouses full of unused, inactive, out-of-date POS. Storage fees compounding month after month. Capital locked in materials that would never reach market. The team was ordering more while existing inventory rotted — because nobody had visibility into what was already there.
Hidden cost: Every new order added to a warehouse already full of waste. The bleeding was silent and continuous.
What brandhub Did
1
Full SKU-level inventory audit. Every item in every warehouse catalogued, tagged with status: active, aging, expired, or redistributable.
2
Dead inventory flagged and actioned. Usable assets redistributed to markets before expiry. Expired stock cleared. Storage footprint reduced immediately.
3
AI demand planning engaged. Right-sized future orders based on actual historical consumption — eliminating overstock at source.
Results
15%
reduction in storage costs
99.7%
on-time delivery rate achieved
100%
inventory visibility across all warehouses
"
We were ordering new product while warehouses were full of product we'd already paid for. brandhub showed us the hole we were pouring money into — and then sealed it.
Case Study 04  ·  Platform Adoption & Simplicity
1,500+
POs placed in under 60 days — zero help desk tickets
🍺 Major U.S. Beer Producer
Platform activity — first 60 days post-launch
1,500+
POs placed
48/50
states shipped to
0
help desk tickets
Who placed orders ~400 individual users
~200 Brand Employees
~200 Distributor Reps
Half of all POs placed directly by distributor salespeople — no brand team in the loop
98%+ field adoption
99%+ on-time in full
0 errors logged
The Problem
A major beer producer needed a system that field sales teams and distributor reps would actually use — not route around. The requirements were non-negotiable: mobile-first, connected to live inventory, user-level budgets and approval rights, and simple enough that anyone could place an order without training. Past systems had all failed on adoption.
The real risk: A platform nobody uses is worse than no platform at all. Local production and off-system spend were the fallback — and they were expensive.
What brandhub Did
1
Deployed a mobile-first ordering portal connected to live inventory feeds — field reps could browse, check stock, and submit orders from their phones in under two minutes.
2
Configured user-level budgets and approval rights for both internal brand employees and external distributor salespeople — each user saw only what they were authorized to order.
3
The platform sold itself. Training sessions and office hours were set up — almost nobody showed. Within 60 days, 400 users across the brand team and distributor network had placed orders independently, across 48 states, with zero errors and zero support requests.
Results
98%+
field adoption rate sustained
99%+
on-time in-full delivery rate
0
errors logged across 1,500+ orders
"
The brandhub team held training sessions, office hours, had a dedicated email, a phone number, a support contact. But it turns out, they didn't need to. One training, 30 minutes was all it took. Within two months our field teams and distributor reps had placed over 1,500 orders across 48 states — completely on their own. The adoption rate on every other system we've tried was our biggest problem. This one, it wasn't even a conversation.
Case Study 05  ·  Freight Transparency
$800K
saved in freight per $10M in display spend
🥃 Major Spirits Company  ·  Display Program Analysis
Freight rate as % of PO — third-party supplier analysis
Competitor A — mid-tier supplier23% freight
$3.6M display PO
Competitor B — mid-tier supplier20% freight
$3.4M display PO
✓ Select Design — brandhub partner15% freight
$9.7M display PO
Select vs. Competitor A on a $10M program:
$1.5M in freight vs. $2.3M — same displays.
$800K
saved in freight alone
The Problem
Display buyers see the product price on every PO. What they don't see — until the invoice arrives — is freight. And freight rates vary significantly by vendor: some ship efficiently; others build margin into logistics. Without visibility into landed costs across suppliers, buyers had no leverage and no way to know how much of their budget was disappearing in transit.
Hidden cost: The display price looked competitive. The total landed cost — once freight was added — told a completely different story.
What brandhub Did
1
Tracked freight as a first-class cost metric. Every vendor's freight rate was captured alongside display pricing — making landed cost the basis for comparison, not just unit price.
2
Ran a cross-vendor freight analysis across the client's full display program, surfacing freight as a percentage of PO for every supplier — ranging from 15% to 23% on the same product categories.
3
Quantified the real-dollar impact. On a $10M display program, the 8-point freight rate difference between the highest- and lowest-cost vendors translated directly to $800K in annual savings — with no change to product, spec, or quality.
Results
$800K
saved in freight per $10M display spend
8pts
lower freight rate vs. highest-cost vendor
100%
landed cost visibility across all vendors
"
We knew freight was a cost. We didn't know how much it varied by vendor — or that we were paying 8 points more than we needed to. Once brandhub surfaced the data, it was an easy call. That's nearly a million dollars in savings we didn't know we had.
